evals.prompt: Create the full SEO report for example.com from every report dated 2026-06-20 under SEO/example.com/. Focus on what we should do next and the strongest ranking opportunities.
evals.expected_output: Selects every date-prefixed source file for the exact date while excluding earlier full-report outputs, reads and reconciles all selected evidence, obtains the bounded current DataForSEO validation, and writes a concise action-first report under SEO/example.com/.
assertions: The exact-date selection includes all regular files beginning 2026-06-20_ and excludes _Full-SEO-Report_ files by default.
The report distinguishes the selected-period evidence from the timestamped current DataForSEO snapshot.
The workflow calls Domain Rank Overview and one bounded Ranked Keywords request for organic positions 4-20 with positive search volume.
The report starts with no more than five prioritized next actions and gives each an owner, steps, effort, timing, KPI, and source references.

evals.prompt: Please combine our latest SEO analyses into a full report and tell me what to fix first.
evals.expected_output: Asks together for the missing domain and exact date or inclusive date range, then waits without reading a guessed directory, making a billable call, or writing a report.
assertions: The workflow asks for both the domain and one ISO date or inclusive ISO date range in one clarification.
No DataForSEO request or output report is produced before the required inputs are supplied.

evals.prompt: Summarize all SEO files for https://www.example.org/shop from 2026-06-01 through 2026-06-15 and save the result below ./client-reports.
evals.expected_output: Normalizes the domain to example.org, selects the inclusive date range below ./client-reports/example.org/, stops without billable calls if no matching files exist, or otherwise reconciles the full source set and writes a range-labeled report.
assertions: The domain is normalized to example.org and the inclusive range is 2026-06-01 through 2026-06-15.
When no file matches, the workflow reports the checked directory and selection and makes no DataForSEO call.
When files match, differing markets or devices remain separate and conflicting scores are not averaged.
